key_insight: AdsMind closes the loop between LLM-based proposal and MLFF relaxation feedback to self-correct adsorption-structure guesses and reliably find low-energy surface configurations.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more dependable catalyst-surface screening by reducing costly search overhead before final DFT validation.
method: Multi-agent LLM system proposes adsorption configurations.
MLFF relaxations provide physics-grounded feedback for error correction.
Closed-loop iteration improves search reliability across backends.
evidence_signal: Across four LLM backends, AdsMind achieved 100% success on AA20 and 98.8% on OCD-GMAE62, using only 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case, with DFT checks on six AA20 systems confirming correct adsorption-energy sign.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality and domain coverage of the MLFF and on careful validation for chemistries outside the benchmark set.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
